I'll help you list the IDs of all unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project and count them. First, let me discover the structure of the sentry_issues table to understand how to filter by project

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues'"}

text: Great! I can see there's a `project` column and a `status` column. Now I'll query for unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, level, count, status, project FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved' ORDER BY id"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"SENTRY-3002","title":"5xx: CSV export truncates large accounts","level":"error","count":838,"status":"unresolved","project":"billing"},{"id":"SENTRY-3015","title":"OOMKilled: SSO SCIM provisioning errors","level":"warning","count":524,"status":"unresolved","project":"billing"},{"id":…(+919)

text: Now let me count them to provide the exact number

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved'"}
